blocks.text: DTC P2127: Gas pedal Pedal Position Sensor 2 voltage low

The accelerator pedal position sensor is a component of the DBW system that converts the accelerator pedal position into an electrical signal, and the PGM-FI ECU controls the throttle actuator to synchronize the accelerator pedal position with the throttle valve position based on the signal from the accelerator pedal position sensor. The PGM-FI ECU controls the throttle actuator to synchronize the throttle pedal position and throttle valve position. If the accelerator pedal position sensor 2 output voltage remains below a predetermined voltage for a predetermined period of time, the PGM-FI ECU judges a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: 24-hour (e.g. observation, operation (of a machine), care (of patient))
Detection order (of the DTC in question)
1D/C (single detection method), PGM-FI warning light: On
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed
Ignition switch/ ENGINE START STOP switch
DTC requesting the prohibition of detection of the relevant DTC

blocks.text: Accelerator Pedal Position Sensor 2 output voltage [AP Sensor 2] remains below 0.2V for 0.2 seconds or longer.

items.text: Disconnection of cord (APS2 line) between PGM-FI ECU and accelerator pedal position sensor 2
Ground fault in cord (APS2 line) between PGM-FI ECU and accelerator pedal position sensor 2
Disconnection of cord (VCC line) between PGM-FI ECU and accelerator pedal position sensor 2

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
